title: A Study on the Impact of Fault localization Granularity for Repository-Scale Code Repair Tasks
summary: Study investigates how fault localization granularity (line, function, or file-level) impacts automatic code repair at repository scale using the SWE-Bench-Mini dataset. Function-level granularity achieves the highest repair rates, though optimal granularity may be task-dependent.
content:
arXiv:2604.00167v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Automatic program repair can be a challenging task, especially when resolving complex issues at a repository-level, which often involves issue reproduction, fault localization, code repair, testing and validation. Issues of this scale can be commonly found in popular GitHub repositories or datasets that are derived from them.
  Some repository-level approaches separate localization and repair into distinct phases. Where this is the case, the fault localization approaches vary in terms of the granularity of localization. Where the impact of granularity is explored to some degree for smaller datasets, not all isolate this issue from the separate question of localization accuracy by testing code repair under the assumption of perfect fault localization. To the best of the authors' knowledge, no repository-scale studies have explicitly investigated granularity under this assumption, nor conducted a systematic empirical comparison of granularity levels in isolation.
  We propose a framework for performing such tests by modifying the localization phase of the Agentless framework to retrieve ground-truth localization data and include this as context in the prompt fed to the repair phase. We show that under this configuration and as a generalization over the SWE-Bench-Mini dataset, function-level granularity yields the highest repair rate against line-level and file-level. However, a deeper dive suggests that the ideal granularity may in fact be task dependent.
  This study is not intended to improve on the state-of-the-art, nor do we intend for results to be compared against any complete agentic frameworks. Rather, we present a proof of concept for investigating how fault localization may impact automatic code repair in repository-scale scenarios. We present preliminary findings to this end and encourage further research into this relationship between the two phases.

zh.title: 故障定位粒度对仓库规模代码修复任务影响的研究
zh.summary: 研究调查了故障定位粒度（行、函数或文件级别）如何影响使用SWE-Bench-Mini数据集的仓库规模自动代码修复。函数级粒度实现最高的修复率，尽管最优粒度可能取决于任务。

fr.title: Une étude sur l'impact de la granularité de la localisation des défauts pour les tâches de réparation de code à l'échelle du référentiel
fr.summary: L'étude enquête sur la façon dont la granularité de la localisation des défauts affecte la réparation automatique du code à l'échelle du référentiel à l'aide de l'ensemble de données SWE-Bench-Mini. La granularité au niveau de la fonction atteint les taux de réparation les plus élevés, bien que la granularité optimale puisse dépendre de la tâche.

de.title: Eine Studie zum Einfluss der Fehlerlokaliserungsgranularität auf Coderepair-Aufgaben im Repository-Maßstab
de.summary: Die Studie untersucht, wie die Granularität der Fehlerlokaliserungszahl die automatische Coderepair im Repository-Maßstab unter Verwendung des SWE-Bench-Mini-Datensatzes beeinflusst. Die Granularität auf Funktionsebene erreicht die höchsten Reparaturquoten, obwohl die optimale Granularität aufgabenabhängig sein kann.

es.title: Un estudio sobre el impacto de la granularidad de localización de defectos para tareas de reparación de código a escala de repositorio
es.summary: El estudio investiga cómo la granularidad de la localización de defectos impacta la reparación automática de código a escala de repositorio utilizando el conjunto de datos SWE-Bench-Mini. La granularidad a nivel de función logra las tasas de reparación más altas, aunque la granularidad óptima puede depender de la tarea.

title: Neural-Assisted in-Motion Self-Heading Alignment
summary: Proposes a neural network approach for rapid initial heading estimation on autonomous ocean platforms, replacing traditional model-based methods. Achieves 53% accuracy improvement and 67% reduction in alignment time.
content:
arXiv:2604.00168v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Autonomous platforms operating in the oceans require accurate navigation to successfully complete their mission. In this regard, the initial heading estimation accuracy and the time required to achieve it play a critical role. The initial heading is traditionally estimated by model-based approaches employing orientation decomposition. However, methods such as the dual vector decomposition and optimized attitude decomposition achieve satisfactory heading accuracy only after long alignment times. To allow rapid and accurate initial heading estimation, we propose an end-to-end, model-free, neural-assisted framework using the same inputs as the model-based approaches. Our proposed approach was trained and evaluated on real-world dataset captured by an autonomous surface vehicle. Our approach shows a significant accuracy improvement over the model-based approaches achieving an average absolute error improvement of 53%. Additionally, our proposed approach was able to reduce the alignment time by up to 67%. Thus, by employing our proposed approach, the reduction in alignment time and improved accuracy allow for a shorter deployment time of an autonomous platform and increased navigation accuracy during the mission.

zh.title: 神经辅助在运动自航向对齐
zh.summary: 提出了一种用于自主海洋平台快速初始航向估计的神经网络方法，取代了传统的基于模型的方法。实现了53%的准确度提高和67%的对齐时间减少。

fr.title: Alignement d'en-tête autonome assisté par réseau de neurones en mouvement
fr.summary: Propose une approche de réseau de neurones pour l'estimation rapide de l'en-tête initial sur les plateformes océaniques autonomes, remplaçant les méthodes traditionnelles basées sur des modèles. Réalise une amélioration de la précision de 53% et une réduction du temps d'alignement de 67%.

de.title: Neuronale unterstützte In-Motion-Selbstrichtungsausrichtung
de.summary: Schlägt einen neuronalen Netzwerkansatz für die schnelle Anfangskursschätzung auf autonomen Ozeanplattformen vor und ersetzt herkömmliche modellbasierte Methoden. Erreicht eine Verbesserung der Genauigkeit um 53% und eine Verringerung der Ausrichtungszeit um 67%.

es.title: Alineación autónoma de rumbo asistida por redes neuronales en movimiento
es.summary: Propone un enfoque de red neuronal para la estimación rápida del rumbo inicial en plataformas oceánicas autónomas, reemplazando métodos tradicionales basados en modelos. Logra una mejora de precisión del 53% y una reducción del tiempo de alineación del 67%.

title: Unified Architecture Metamodel of Information Systems Developed by Generative AI
summary: Proposes a unified architectural framework for LLM-oriented applications that enables consistent code-to-documentation-to-code transformations. Demonstrates improved stability and quality of generated artifacts when using structured architectural diagrams as context.
content:
arXiv:2604.00171v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: The rapid development of AI and LLMs has driven new methods of SDLC, in which a large portion of code, technical, and business documentation is generated automatically. However, since there is no single architectural framework that can provide consistent, repeatable transformations across different representation layers of information systems, such systems remain fragmented in their system representation. This study explores the problem of creating a unified architecture for LLM-oriented applications based on selected architectural frameworks by SMEs. A framework structure is proposed that covers some key types of architectural diagrams and supports a closed cycle of transformations, such as: "Code to Documentation to Code". The key architectural diagrams are split equally between main architectural layers: high-layer (business and domain understanding), middle-layer (system architecture), and low-layer (developer-layer architecture). Each architectural layer still contains some abstraction layers, which make it more flexible and better fit the requirements of design principles and architectural patterns. The conducted experiments demonstrated the stable quality of generated documentation and code when using a structured architectural context in the form of architectural diagrams. The results confirm that the proposed unified architecture metamodel can serve as an effective interface between humans and models, improving the accuracy, stability, and repeatability of LLM generation. However, the selected set of architectural diagrams should be optimised to avoid redundancy between some diagrams, and some diagrams should be updated to represent extra contextual orchestration. This work demonstrates measurable improvements for a new generation of intelligent tools that automate the SDLC and enable a comprehensive architecture compatible with AI-driven development.

zh.title: 生成式AI开发的信息系统统一架构元模型
zh.summary: 提出了一个针对LLM导向应用的统一架构框架，实现了代码到文档到代码的一致性转换。当使用结构化架构图作为上下文时，演示了生成工件的改进的稳定性和质量。

fr.title: Métamodèle d'architecture unifiée des systèmes d'information développés par l'IA générative
fr.summary: Propose un cadre architectural unifié pour les applications orientées LLM qui permet des transformations cohérentes de code à documentation et de documentation à code. Démontre une stabilité et une qualité améliorées des artefacts générés lors de l'utilisation de diagrammes architecturaux structurés comme contexte.

de.title: Einheitliches Architektur-Metamodell von Informationssystemen, die durch generative KI entwickelt wurden
de.summary: Schlägt ein einheitliches Architektur-Framework für LLM-orientierte Anwendungen vor, das konsistente Code-zu-Dokumentation-zu-Code-Transformationen ermöglicht. Zeigt verbesserte Stabilität und Qualität generierter Artefakte bei Verwendung strukturierter Architekturdiagramme als Kontext.

es.title: Metamodelo de arquitectura unificada de sistemas de información desarrollados por IA generativa
es.summary: Propone un marco arquitectónico unificado para aplicaciones orientadas a LLM que permite transformaciones consistentes de código a documentación y de documentación a código. Demuestra una estabilidad y calidad mejoradas de los artefactos generados cuando se utilizan diagramas arquitectónicos estructurados como contexto.

title: NFC based inventory control system for secure and efficient communication
summary: Presents NFC-based alternative to traditional barcodes for inventory management, offering improved security and reliability across product types. Prototype demonstrates receipt generation through NFC communication at checkout.
content:
arXiv:2604.00181v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: This paper brings up this idea of using Near Field Communication (NFC) for inventory control system instead of using traditional barcodes. NFC because of its high security, ease of use and efficiency can be very suitable for systems like inventory control. In traditional inventory control systems, each product has a barcode pasted on it, which is vulnerable to attacks as barcodes are open and have no security. Furthermore, barcodes are prone to damages and can be unreliable when pasted on different types of products e.g. hot and frozen products, circular shaped products and irregular shaped products like clothes etc. NFC on the other hand is very efficient, secure and reliable when it comes to short-range wireless communication. In this paper we will present our prototype for the inventory control system of an electronic store in which each product has a passive NFC tag pasted to it. When a customer buys a product the receipt of the product is generated using NFC between the NFC passive tag on the product and NFC enabled device (e.g. smart phone or reader) at the cash counter.

zh.title: 基于NFC的安全高效通信库存控制系统
zh.summary: 介绍了NFC技术作为传统条形码的替代方案用于库存管理，提供跨产品类型的改进安全性和可靠性。原型演示通过NFC通信在结账时生成收据。

fr.title: Système de contrôle d'inventaire basé sur NFC pour une communication sécurisée et efficace
fr.summary: Présente une alternative basée sur la technologie NFC aux codes-barres traditionnels pour la gestion des stocks, offrant une sécurité et une fiabilité améliorées pour tous les types de produits. Le prototype démontre la génération de reçus par communication NFC au point de vente.

de.title: NFC-basiertes Bestandskontrollsystem für sichere und effiziente Kommunikation
de.summary: Präsentiert eine NFC-basierte Alternative zu herkömmlichen Barcodes für die Bestandsverwaltung und bietet verbesserte Sicherheit und Zuverlässigkeit für verschiedene Produkttypen. Der Prototyp demonstriert die Quittungsgenerierung durch NFC-Kommunikation beim Checkout.

es.title: Sistema de control de inventario basado en NFC para comunicación segura y eficiente
es.summary: Presenta una alternativa basada en NFC a los códigos de barras tradicionales para la gestión de inventario, ofreciendo mayor seguridad y confiabilidad en diferentes tipos de productos. El prototipo demuestra la generación de recibos a través de comunicación NFC en el punto de venta.

title: Agentic AI and Occupational Displacement: A Multi-Regional Task Exposure Analysis of Emerging Labor Market Disruption
summary: Extends labor market analysis to measure displacement risk from autonomous AI agents executing entire workflows. Finds 93.2% of analyzed occupations in major US tech regions face moderate-to-high risk by 2030, while identifying 17 emerging roles in AI governance and collaboration.
content:
arXiv:2604.00186v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: This paper extends the Acemoglu-Restrepo task exposure framework to address the labor market effects of agentic artificial intelligence systems: autonomous AI agents capable of completing entire occupational workflows rather than discrete tasks. Unlike prior automation technologies that substitute for individual subtasks, agentic AI systems execute end-to-end workflows involving multi-step reasoning, tool invocation, and autonomous decision-making, substantially expanding occupational displacement risk beyond what existing task-level analyses capture. We introduce the Agentic Task Exposure (ATE) score, a composite measure computed algorithmically from O*NET task data using calibrated adoption parameters--not a regression estimate--incorporating AI capability scores, workflow coverage factors, and logistic adoption velocity. Applying the ATE framework across five major US technology regions (Seattle-Tacoma, San Francisco Bay Area, Austin, New York, and Boston) over a 2025-2030 horizon, we find that 93.2% of the 236 analyzed occupations across six information-intensive SOC groups (financial, legal, healthcare, healthcare support, sales, and administrative/clerical) cross the moderate-risk threshold (ATE >= 0.35) in Tier 1 regions by 2030, with credit analysts, judges, and sustainability specialists reaching ATE scores of 0.43-0.47. We simultaneously identify seventeen emerging occupational categories benefiting from reinstatement effects, concentrated in human-AI collaboration, AI governance, and domain-specific AI operations roles. Our findings carry implications for workforce transition policy, regional economic planning, and the temporal dynamics of labor market adjustment

zh.title: 代理型AI与职业流离：新兴劳动力市场混乱的多地区任务暴露分析
zh.summary: 将劳动力市场分析扩展到衡量自主AI代理执行完整工作流的流离风险。发现主要美国科技地区93.2%的被分析职业面临2030年前中等至高风险，同时确定了17个AI治理和协作中出现的新兴职业。

fr.title: IA Agentive et Déplacement Professionnel : Une Analyse Multi-Régionale de l'Exposition aux Tâches de la Perturbation du Marché du Travail Émergent
fr.summary: Étend l'analyse du marché du travail pour mesurer le risque de déplacement des agents IA autonomes exécutant des flux de travail complets. Constate que 93,2% des professions analysées dans les principales régions technologiques américaines font face à un risque modéré à élevé d'ici 2030, tout en identifiant 17 rôles émergents en gouvernance et collaboration de l'IA.

de.title: Agentic AI und Berufliche Verdrängung: Eine Multi-Regional-Analyse der Aufgabenexposition zur Störung des entstehenden Arbeitsmarktes
de.summary: Erweitert die Arbeitsmarktanalyse um Verdrängungsrisiken durch autonome KI-Agenten, die komplette Workflows ausführen. Ermittelt, dass 93,2% der analysierten Berufe in großen US-Tech-Regionen bis 2030 mäßig bis hohes Risiko aufweisen, und identifiziert 17 neue Rollen in KI-Governance und Zusammenarbeit.

es.title: IA Agentiva y Desplazamiento Ocupacional: Un Análisis Multi-Regional de Exposición a Tareas de Perturbación del Mercado Laboral Emergente
es.summary: Extiende el análisis del mercado laboral para medir el riesgo de desplazamiento de agentes de IA autónomos que ejecutan flujos de trabajo completos. Encuentra que el 93,2% de las ocupaciones analizadas en las principales regiones tecnológicas de EE.UU. enfrentan un riesgo moderado a alto para 2030, mientras identifica 17 roles emergentes en gobernanza e colaboración de IA.

title: Explainable AI for Blind and Low-Vision Users: Navigating Trust, Modality, and Interpretability in the Agentic Era
summary: Examines accessibility barriers for blind and low-vision users in AI-driven systems, particularly as AI shifts to autonomous agents making multi-step decisions. Proposes research agenda for multimodal, blame-aware explanation design.
content:
arXiv:2604.00187v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) is critical for ensuring trust and accountability, yet its development remains predominantly visual. For blind and low-vision (BLV) users, the lack of accessible explanations creates a fundamental barrier to the independent use of AI-driven assistive technologies. This problem intensifies as AI systems shift from single-query tools into autonomous agents that take multi-step actions and make consequential decisions across extended task horizons, where a single undetected error can propagate irreversibly before any feedback is available. This paper investigates the unique XAI requirements of the BLV community through a comprehensive analysis of user interviews and contemporary research. By examining usage patterns across environmental perception and decision support, we identify a significant modality gap. Empirical evidence suggests that while BLV users highly value conversational explanations, they frequently experience "self-blame" for AI failures. The paper concludes with a research agenda for accessible Explainable AI in agentic systems, advocating for multimodal interfaces, blame-aware explanation design, and participatory development.

zh.title: 为盲人和低视力用户的可解释AI：在代理时代导航信任、模态和可解释性
zh.summary: 检查盲人和低视力用户在AI驱动系统中的可访问性障碍，特别是当AI转向自主代理做出多步骤决策时。提出多模态、责任感知解释设计的研究议程。

fr.title: IA Explicable pour les Utilisateurs Aveugles et Malvoyants : Naviguer la Confiance, la Modalité et l'Interprétabilité à l'Ère Agentive
fr.summary: Examine les barrières d'accessibilité pour les utilisateurs aveugles et malvoyants dans les systèmes pilotés par l'IA, particulièrement alors que l'IA bascule vers des agents autonomes prenant des décisions multi-étapes. Propose un agenda de recherche pour une conception d'explications multimodales et conscientes des responsabilités.

de.title: Erklärbare KI für blinde und sehbehinderte Benutzer: Navigation von Vertrauen, Modalität und Interpretierbarkeit im Agentic-Zeitalter
de.summary: Untersucht Zugänglichkeitsbarrieren für blinde und sehbehinderte Benutzer in KI-gestützten Systemen, besonders wenn KI zu autonomen Agenten mit mehrstufigen Entscheidungen übergeht. Schlägt eine Forschungsagenda für multimodale, verantwortungsbewusste Erklärungsdesigns vor.

es.title: IA Explicable para Usuarios Ciegos y con Baja Visión: Navegando Confianza, Modalidad e Interpretabilidad en la Era Agentiva
es.summary: Examina las barreras de accesibilidad para usuarios ciegos y con baja visión en sistemas impulsados por IA, particularmente a medida que la IA se desplaza hacia agentes autónomos que toman decisiones de múltiples pasos. Propone una agenda de investigación para diseño de explicaciones multimodales y conscientes de la responsabilidad.

title: Making Sense of AI Agents Hype: Adoption, Architectures, and Takeaways from Practitioners
summary: Reviews 138 practitioner conference talks documenting how companies adopt agent-based architectures in real-world practice. Identifies recurring architectural patterns and application domains for implementing LLM-driven agentic systems.
content:
arXiv:2604.00189v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: To support practitioners in understanding how agentic systems are designed in real-world industrial practice, we present a review of practitioner conference talks on AI agents. We analyzed 138 recorded talks to examine how companies adopt agent-based architectures (Objective 1), identify recurring architectural strategies and patterns (Objective 2), and analyze application domains and technologies used to implement and operate LLM-driven agentic systems (Objective 3).

zh.title: 理解AI代理炒作：从业者的采纳、架构和启示
zh.summary: 审视138场从业者会议演讲，记录企业如何在实际应用中采纳基于代理的架构。识别实现LLM驱动的代理系统的循环架构模式和应用领域。

fr.title: Comprendre le battage médiatique autour des agents IA : adoption, architectures et enseignements des praticiens
fr.summary: Passe en revue 138 présentations de conférences de praticiens documentant comment les entreprises adoptent des architectures basées sur des agents en pratique réelle. Identifie les modèles architecturaux récurrents et les domaines d'application pour implémenter les systèmes agentiques basés sur LLM.

de.title: Der AI-Agenten-Hype verstehen: Adoption, Architekturen und Erkenntnisse von Praktikern
de.summary: Überprüft 138 Vorträge von Praktikern auf Konferenzen, die dokumentieren, wie Unternehmen agentenbasierte Architekturen in der Praxis einführen. Identifiziert wiederkehrende Architekturmuster und Anwendungsbereiche für die Implementierung von LLM-gesteuerten agentengesteuerten Systemen.

es.title: Comprendiendo la expectativa de los agentes de IA: adopción, arquitecturas y lecciones de los profesionales
es.summary: Revisa 138 charlas de conferencias de profesionales documentando cómo las empresas adoptan arquitecturas basadas en agentes en la práctica real. Identifica patrones arquitectónicos recurrentes y dominios de aplicación para implementar sistemas agenticos impulsados por LLM.

title: QUEST: A robust attention formulation using query-modulated spherical attention
summary: Proposes QUEST attention mechanism that constrains keys to hyperspherical space while allowing flexible control of attention sharpness. Demonstrates improved training stability and robustness to corruptions compared to standard softmax attention.
content:
arXiv:2604.00199v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: The Transformer model architecture has become one of the most widely used in deep learning and the attention mechanism is at its core. The standard attention formulation uses a softmax operation applied to a scaled dot product between query and key vectors. We explore the role played by norms of the queries and keys, which can cause training instabilities when they arbitrarily increase. We demonstrate how this can happen even in simple Transformer models, in the presence of easy-to-learn spurious patterns in the data. We propose a new attention formulation, QUEry-modulated Spherical aTtention (QUEST), that constrains the keys to a hyperspherical latent space, while still allowing individual tokens to flexibly control the sharpness of the attention distribution. QUEST can be easily used as a drop-in replacement for standard attention. We focus on vision applications while also exploring other domains to highlight the method's generality. We show that (1) QUEST trains without instabilities and (2) produces models with improved performance (3) that are robust to data corruptions and adversarial attacks.

zh.title: QUEST：使用查询调制球面注意力的健壮注意力公式
zh.summary: 提议QUEST注意力机制，将键约束在超球面空间中，同时允许灵活控制注意力锐度。与标准softmax注意力相比，展示了改进的训练稳定性和对损坏的鲁棒性。

fr.title: QUEST : une formulation d'attention robuste utilisant l'attention sphérique modulée par la requête
fr.summary: Propose un mécanisme d'attention QUEST qui contraint les clés à l'espace hypersphérique tout en permettant un contrôle flexible de l'acuité de l'attention. Démontre une stabilité d'entraînement améliorée et une robustesse aux corruptions par rapport à l'attention softmax standard.

de.title: QUEST: Eine robuste Aufmerksamkeitsformulierung mit abfragemodulierter sphärischer Aufmerksamkeit
de.summary: Schlägt einen QUEST-Aufmerksamkeitsmechanismus vor, der Schlüssel auf einen hypersphärischen Raum beschränkt und gleichzeitig flexible Kontrolle der Aufmerksamkeitsschärfe ermöglicht. Demonstriert verbesserte Trainingsstabilität und Robustheit gegenüber Beschädigungen im Vergleich zu standardmäßiger Softmax-Aufmerksamkeit.

es.title: QUEST: Una formulación de atención robusta utilizando atención esférica modulada por consulta
es.summary: Propone un mecanismo de atención QUEST que limita las claves al espacio hiperesferico mientras permite control flexible de la agudeza de atención. Demuestra estabilidad de entrenamiento mejorada y robustez a corrupciones en comparación con la atención softmax estándar.

title: Diversity-Aware Reverse Kullback-Leibler Divergence for Large Language Model Distillation
summary: Identifies structural limitations in reverse KL divergence for LLM distillation that drive overconfident predictions and reduce output diversity. Proposes Diversity-aware RKL that improves non-target class supervision while maintaining optimization benefits.
content:
arXiv:2604.00223v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Reverse Kullback-Leibler (RKL) divergence has recently emerged as the preferred objective for large language model (LLM) distillation, consistently outperforming forward KL (FKL), particularly in regimes with large vocabularies and significant teacher-student capacity mismatch, where RKL focuses learning on dominant modes rather than enforcing dense alignment. However, RKL introduces a structural limitation that drives the student toward overconfident predictions. We first provide an analysis of RKL by decomposing its gradients into target and non-target components, and show that non-target gradients consistently push the target logit upward even when the student already matches the teacher, thereby reducing output diversity. In addition, RKL provides weak supervision over non-target classes, leading to poor tail alignment. To address these issues, we propose Diversity-aware RKL (DRKL), which removes this gradient effect and strengthens non-target supervision while preserving the optimization benefits of RKL. Extensive experiments across datasets and model families demonstrate that DRKL consistently outperforms FKL, RKL, and other state-of-the-art distillation objectives, achieving better performance and a superior fidelity-diversity trade-off.

zh.title: 多样性感知的反向Kullback-Leibler散度用于大型语言模型蒸馏
zh.summary: 确定反向KL散度在LLM蒸馏中的结构限制，导致过度自信的预测并降低输出多样性。提议多样性感知RKL，改进非目标类监督，同时保持优化优势。

fr.title: Divergence inverse de Kullback-Leibler consciente de la diversité pour la distillation de grands modèles de langage
fr.summary: Identifie les limitations structurelles de la divergence KL inverse pour la distillation LLM qui conduisent à des prédictions trop confiantes et réduisent la diversité des résultats. Propose une RKL consciente de la diversité qui améliore la supervision des classes non-cibles tout en maintenant les avantages d'optimisation.

de.title: Diversitätsbewusste umgekehrte Kullback-Leibler-Divergenz für die Destillation großer Sprachmodelle
de.summary: Identifiziert strukturelle Einschränkungen in der umgekehrten KL-Divergenz für LLM-Destillation, die zu überconfidenten Vorhersagen führen und die Ausgabenvielfalt verringern. Schlägt eine diversitätsbewusste RKL vor, die die Überwachung von Nicht-Zielklassen verbessert und gleichzeitig Optimierungsvorteile erhält.

es.title: Divergencia inversa de Kullback-Leibler consciente de la diversidad para destilación de modelos de lenguaje grande
es.summary: Identifica limitaciones estructurales en la divergencia KL inversa para destilación de LLM que impulsan predicciones excesivamente confiadas y reducen la diversidad de salida. Propone RKL consciente de diversidad que mejora la supervisión de clases no objetivo mientras mantiene beneficios de optimización.

title: MAC-Attention: a Match-Amend-Complete Scheme for Fast and Accurate Attention Computation
summary: Introduces MAC-Attention, which accelerates long-context LLM decoding by reusing prior attention computations for semantically similar queries. Achieves up to 60% latency reduction and 2.6x end-to-end speedup while maintaining full-attention quality.
content:
arXiv:2604.00235v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Long-context decoding in LLMs is IO-bound: each token re-reads an ever-growing KV cache. Prior accelerations cut bytes via compression, which lowers fidelity, or selection/eviction, which restricts what remains accessible, and both can degrade delayed recall and long-form generation. We introduce MAC-Attention, a fidelity- and access-preserving alternative that accelerates decoding by reusing prior attention computations for semantically similar recent queries. It starts with a match stage that performs pre-RoPE L2 matching over a short local window; an amend stage rectifies the reused attention by recomputing a small band near the match boundary; and a complete stage fuses the rectified results with fresh attention computed on the KV tail through a numerically stable merge. On a match hit, the compute and bandwidth complexity is constant regardless of context length. The method is model-agnostic and composes with IO-aware kernels, paged-KV managers, and MQA/GQA. Across LongBench v2 (120K), RULER (120K), and LongGenBench (16K continuous generation), compared to the latest FlashInfer library, MAC-Attention reduces KV accesses by up to 99%, cuts token generation latency by over 60% at 128K, and achieves over 14.3x attention-phase speedups, up to 2.6x end-to-end, while maintaining full-attention quality. By reusing computation, MAC-Attention delivers long-context inference that is both fast and faithful. Code is available here: https://github.com/YJHMITWEB/MAC-Attention.git

zh.title: MAC-Attention：用于快速准确注意力计算的匹配-修正-完成方案
zh.summary: 介绍了MAC-Attention，它通过为语义相似的查询重用先前的注意力计算来加速长上下文LLM解码。实现了高达60%的延迟减少和2.6倍的端到端加速，同时保持了完整注意力质量。

fr.title: MAC-Attention : Un schéma Match-Amend-Complete pour le calcul rapide et précis de l'attention
fr.summary: Présente MAC-Attention, qui accélère le décodage des LLM en contexte long en réutilisant les calculs d'attention antérieurs pour les requêtes sémantiquement similaires. Réalise une réduction de latence jusqu'à 60% et une accélération de bout en bout de 2,6x tout en maintenant la qualité de l'attention complète.

de.title: MAC-Attention: Ein Match-Amend-Complete-Schema für schnelle und genaue Attention-Berechnung
de.summary: Stellt MAC-Attention vor, das die Dekodierung von langkontextigen LLMs beschleunigt, indem frühere Attention-Berechnungen für semantisch ähnliche Abfragen wiederverwendet werden. Erreicht eine Latenzreduktion von bis zu 60% und eine End-to-End-Beschleunigung von 2,6x bei Beibehaltung der vollständigen Attention-Qualität.

es.title: MAC-Attention: Un esquema Match-Amend-Complete para la computación rápida y precisa de atención
es.summary: Introduce MAC-Attention, que acelera la decodificación de LLM de contexto largo reutilizando cálculos de atención previos para consultas semánticamente similares. Logra una reducción de latencia de hasta el 60% y una aceleración de extremo a extremo de 2,6x manteniendo la calidad de atención completa.

title: AI-Mediated Explainable Regulation for Justice
summary: Proposes using distributed AI to generate explainable, adaptable regulatory recommendations based on aggregated stakeholder preferences. Addresses problems with current regulatory processes including static rules, lack of transparency, and undue corporate influence.
content:
arXiv:2604.00237v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Present practice of deciding on regulation faces numerous problems that make adopted regulations static, unexplained, unduly influenced by powerful interest groups, and stained with a perception of illegitimacy. These well-known problems with the regulatory process can lead to injustice and have substantial negative effects on society and democracy. We discuss a new approach that utilizes distributed artificial intelligence (AI) to make a regulatory recommendation that is explainable and adaptable by design. We outline the main components of a system that can implement this approach and show how it would resolve the problems with the present regulatory system. This approach models and reasons about stakeholder preferences with separate preference models, while it aggregates these preferences in a value sensitive way. Such recommendations can be updated due to changes in facts or in values and are inherently explainable. We suggest how stakeholders can make their preferences known to the system and how they can verify whether they were properly considered in the regulatory decision. The resulting system promises to support regulatory justice, legitimacy, and compliance.

zh.title: 用于司法的AI中介可解释监管
zh.summary: 提议使用分布式AI基于汇总的利益相关者偏好生成可解释的、可适应的监管建议。解决当前监管流程的问题，包括静态规则、缺乏透明度和不适当的企业影响。

fr.title: Réglementation explicable médiatisée par l'IA pour la justice
fr.summary: Propose d'utiliser l'IA distribuée pour générer des recommandations réglementaires explicables et adaptables basées sur les préférences agrégées des parties prenantes. Résout les problèmes des processus réglementaires actuels, notamment les règles statiques, le manque de transparence et l'influence indue des entreprises.

de.title: KI-vermittelte erklärbare Regulierung für Gerechtigkeit
de.summary: Schlägt die Verwendung verteilter KI vor, um erklärbare, anpassbare Regulierungsempfehlungen basierend auf aggregierten Stakeholder-Präferenzen zu generieren. Behebt Probleme bei aktuellen Regulierungsprozessen, einschließlich statischer Regeln, Mangel an Transparenz und unangemessenem Unternehmenseinfluss.

es.title: Regulación explicable mediada por IA para la justicia
es.summary: Propone usar IA distribuida para generar recomendaciones regulatorias explicables y adaptables basadas en preferencias agregadas de las partes interesadas. Aborda problemas con procesos regulatorios actuales incluyendo reglas estáticas, falta de transparencia e influencia corporativa indebida.

title: Softmax gradient policy for variance minimization and risk-averse multi armed bandits
summary: Develops algorithm for multi-armed bandit problems focused on minimizing variance rather than maximizing expected reward. Provides convergence guarantees and practical guidance for risk-aware decision-making scenarios.
content:
arXiv:2604.00241v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Algorithms for the Multi-Armed Bandit (MAB) problem play a central role in sequential decision-making and have been extensively explored both theoretically and numerically. While most classical approaches aim to identify the arm with the highest expected reward, we focus on a risk-aware setting where the goal is to select the arm with the lowest variance, favoring stability over potentially high but uncertain returns. To model the decision process, we consider a softmax parameterization of the policy; we propose a new algorithm to select the minimal variance (or minimal risk) arm and prove its convergence under natural conditions. The algorithm constructs an unbiased estimate of the objective by using two independent draws from the current's arm distribution. We provide numerical experiments that illustrate the practical behavior of these algorithms and offer guidance on implementation choices. The setting also covers general risk-aware problems where there is a trade-off between maximizing the average reward and minimizing its variance.

zh.title: 用于方差最小化和风险规避多臂老虎机的Softmax梯度策略
zh.summary: 开发了多臂老虎机问题的算法，专注于最小化方差而不是最大化预期奖励。提供了收敛保证和风险意识决策场景的实际指导。

fr.title: Politique de gradient Softmax pour la minimisation de la variance et les bandits multi-bras averses au risque
fr.summary: Développe un algorithme pour les problèmes de bandit multi-bras axé sur la minimisation de la variance plutôt que sur la maximisation de la récompense attendue. Fournit des garanties de convergence et des conseils pratiques pour les scénarios de prise de décision conscient des risques.

de.title: Softmax-Gradienten-Richtlinie zur Varianzminimierung und risikoaverse Multi-Armed Bandits
de.summary: Entwickelt einen Algorithmus für Multi-Armed-Bandit-Probleme, der sich auf die Varianzminimierung anstelle der Maximierung der erwarteten Belohnung konzentriert. Bietet Konvergenzgarantien und praktische Anleitungen für risikoaware Entscheidungsszenarios.

es.title: Política de gradiente Softmax para minimización de varianza y bandidos multi-brazos adversos al riesgo
es.summary: Desarrolla un algoritmo para problemas de bandido de múltiples brazos enfocado en minimizar la varianza en lugar de maximizar la recompensa esperada. Proporciona garantías de convergencia y orientación práctica para escenarios de toma de decisiones conscientes del riesgo.

title: REM-CTX: Automated Peer Review via Reinforcement Learning with Auxiliary Context
summary: Introduces REM-CTX, an RL-based system that enhances manuscript peer review by incorporating figures and external scholarly signals. Outperforms larger commercial models on review quality while improving contextual grounding.
content:
arXiv:2604.00248v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Most automated peer review systems rely on textual manuscript content alone, leaving visual elements such as figures and external scholarly signals underutilized. We introduce REM-CTX, a reinforcement-learning system that incorporates auxiliary context into the review generation process via correspondence-aware reward functions. REM-CTX trains an 8B-parameter language model with Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) and combines a multi-aspect quality reward with two correspondence rewards that explicitly encourage alignment with auxiliary context. Experiments on manuscripts across Computer, Biological, and Physical Sciences show that REM-CTX achieves the highest overall review quality among six baselines, outperforming other systems with substantially larger commercial models, and surpassing the next-best RL baseline across both quality and contextual grounding metrics. Ablation studies confirm that the two correspondence rewards are complementary: each selectively improves its targeted correspondence reward while preserving all quality dimensions, and the full model outperforms all partial variants. Analysis of training dynamics reveals that the criticism aspect is negatively correlated with other metrics during training, suggesting that future studies should group multi-dimension rewards for review generation.

zh.title: REM-CTX：通过强化学习和辅助上下文进行自动化同行评审
zh.summary: 介绍REM-CTX，一个基于强化学习的系统，通过整合图表和外部学术信号来改进手稿同行评审。在评审质量上超越了更大的商业模型，同时改善了上下文依据。

fr.title: REM-CTX : Évaluation par les pairs automatisée via l'apprentissage par renforcement avec contexte auxiliaire
fr.summary: Présente REM-CTX, un système basé sur l'RL qui améliore l'examen par les pairs des manuscrits en incorporant des figures et des signaux érudits externes. Surpasse les modèles commerciaux plus grands en qualité d'examen tout en améliorant l'ancrage contextuel.

de.title: REM-CTX: Automatisierte Peer-Review durch Reinforcement Learning mit Hilfskontext
de.summary: Führt REM-CTX ein, ein auf RL basierendes System, das die Manuskript-Peer-Review durch Einbeziehung von Abbildungen und externen wissenschaftlichen Signalen verbessert. Übertrifft größere kommerzielle Modelle bei der Überprüfungsqualität und verbessert gleichzeitig die kontextuelle Verankerung.

es.title: REM-CTX: Revisión por pares automatizada mediante aprendizaje por refuerzo con contexto auxiliar
es.summary: Presenta REM-CTX, un sistema basado en RL que mejora la revisión por pares de manuscritos incorporando figuras y señales académicas externas. Supera modelos comerciales más grandes en calidad de revisión mientras mejora el anclaje contextual.

title: Hierarchical Apprenticeship Learning from Imperfect Demonstrations with Evolving Rewards
summary: Proposes HALIDE, which learns from imperfect student demonstrations by ranking them hierarchically and modeling evolving reward functions. Demonstrates improved ability to distinguish transient errors from suboptimal strategies in e-learning contexts.
content:
arXiv:2604.00258v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: While apprenticeship learning has shown promise for inducing effective pedagogical policies directly from student interactions in e-learning environments, most existing approaches rely on optimal or near-optimal expert demonstrations under a fixed reward. Real-world student interactions, however, are often inherently imperfect and evolving: students explore, make errors, revise strategies, and refine their goals as understanding develops. In this work, we argue that imperfect student demonstrations are not noise to be discarded, but structured signals-provided their relative quality is ranked. We introduce HALIDE, Hierarchical Apprenticeship Learning from Imperfect Demonstrations with Evolving Rewards, which not only leverages sub-optimal student demonstrations, but ranks them within a hierarchical learning framework. HALIDE models student behavior at multiple levels of abstraction, enabling inference of higher-level intent and strategy from suboptimal actions while explicitly capturing the temporal evolution of student reward functions. By integrating demonstration quality into hierarchical reward inference,HALIDE distinguishes transient errors from suboptimal strategies and meaningful progress toward higher-level learning goals. Our results show that HALIDE more accurately predicts student pedagogical decisions than approaches that rely on optimal trajectories, fixed rewards, or unranked imperfect demonstrations.

zh.title: 来自不完美演示的分层学徒学习与演变奖励
zh.summary: 提出HALIDE，通过分层排序不完美的学生演示并建模演变奖励函数来学习。在电子学习环境中展示了区分瞬时错误和次优策略的改进能力。

fr.title: Apprentissage par apprentissage hiérarchique à partir de démonstrations imparfaites avec récompenses évolutives
fr.summary: Propose HALIDE, qui apprend à partir de démonstrations imparfaites d'étudiants en les classant hiérarchiquement et en modélisant les fonctions de récompense évolutives. Démontre une meilleure capacité à distinguer les erreurs transitoires des stratégies sous-optimales dans les contextes d'apprentissage électronique.

de.title: Hierarchisches Lehrlinglernen aus unvollkommenen Demonstrationen mit sich entwickelnden Belohnungen
de.summary: Schlägt HALIDE vor, das aus unvollkommen Studentendemonstrationen lernt, indem es diese hierarchisch ordnet und Funktionen mit sich entwickelnden Belohnungen modelliert. Demonstriert eine verbesserte Fähigkeit, vorübergehende Fehler von suboptimalen Strategien in E-Learning-Kontexten zu unterscheiden.

es.title: Aprendizaje jerárquico de aprendizaje a partir de demostraciones imperfectas con recompensas evolutivas
es.summary: Propone HALIDE, que aprende de demostraciones imperfectas de estudiantes clasificándolas jerárquicamente y modelando funciones de recompensa evolutivas. Demuestra una capacidad mejorada para distinguir errores transitorios de estrategias subóptimas en contextos de aprendizaje electrónico.

title: LLM Essay Scoring Under Holistic and Analytic Rubrics: Prompt Effects and Bias
summary: Systematic evaluation of LLMs on essay scoring across holistic and analytic rubrics reveals moderate-to-high holistic agreement with humans but systematic negative bias on grammar and conventions. Recommends bias-correction strategy using small validation sets.
content:
arXiv:2604.00259v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Despite growing interest in using Large Language Models (LLMs) for educational assessment, it remains unclear how closely they align with human scoring. We present a systematic evaluation of instruction-tuned LLMs across three open essay-scoring datasets (ASAP 2.0, ELLIPSE, and DREsS) that cover both holistic and analytic scoring. We analyze agreement with human consensus scores, directional bias, and the stability of bias estimates. Our results show that strong open-weight models achieve moderate to high agreement with humans on holistic scoring (Quadratic Weighted Kappa about 0.6), but this does not transfer uniformly to analytic scoring. In particular, we observe large and stable negative directional bias on Lower-Order Concern (LOC) traits, such as Grammar and Conventions, meaning that models often score these traits more harshly than human raters. We also find that concise keyword-based prompts generally outperform longer rubric-style prompts in multi-trait analytic scoring. To quantify the amount of data needed to detect these systematic deviations, we compute the minimum sample size at which a 95% bootstrap confidence interval for the mean bias excludes zero. This analysis shows that LOC bias is often detectable with very small validation sets, whereas Higher-Order Concern (HOC) traits typically require much larger samples. These findings support a bias-correction-first deployment strategy: instead of relying on raw zero-shot scores, systematic score offsets can be estimated and corrected using small human-labeled bias-estimation sets, without requiring large-scale fine-tuning.

zh.title: 整体和解析型评分标准下的LLM论文评分：提示效应和偏见
zh.summary: 对LLM在整体和解析型评分标准下的论文评分进行了系统评估，发现与人工评分在整体上有中等到高度的一致性，但在语法和约定方面存在系统性负偏见。建议使用小验证集的偏见纠正策略。

fr.title: Notation d'essai LLM selon des grilles holistiques et analytiques : effets des invites et biais
fr.summary: L'évaluation systématique des LLM sur la notation d'essai selon les grilles holistiques et analytiques révèle un accord holistique modéré à élevé avec les humains, mais un biais négatif systématique sur la grammaire et les conventions. Recommande une stratégie de correction des biais utilisant de petits ensembles de validation.

de.title: LLM-Essaybenotung nach holistischen und analytischen Bewertungskriterien: Eingabeeffekte und Voreingenommenheit
de.summary: Eine systematische Bewertung von LLMs bei der Essaybenotung anhand holistischer und analytischer Bewertungskriterien zeigt eine mäßig bis hohe holistische Übereinstimmung mit Menschen, aber eine systematische negative Voreingenommenheit bezüglich Grammatik und Konventionen. Empfiehlt eine Bias-Korrekturstrategie mit kleinen Validierungssätzen.

es.title: Calificación de ensayos de LLM bajo rúbricas holísticas y analíticas: efectos de indicación y sesgo
es.summary: La evaluación sistemática de LLM en la calificación de ensayos según rúbricas holísticas y analíticas revela un acuerdo holístico moderado a alto con humanos pero un sesgo negativo sistemático en gramática y convenciones. Recomienda una estrategia de corrección de sesgo utilizando pequeños conjuntos de validación.
